best-screenplay.winner_citation: A build thread with a whole cast of voices — including the head coach stepping up to the mic when the students left him with nothing to complain about.
best-screenplay.quote: Hello friends of Stuy Robotics! For those who don't know me, I'm Joe Blay and I am the head coach of the Stuyvesant Robotics program. With all the students doing such a good job posting on OA regularly, there is nothing for me to post about.
best-screenplay.presenter: For a season-long story with real voice, real stakes, and a surprise guest narrator…

nominees.citation: Turned 'shooting on the move' into a page-turner — "it's not just hard, it's writing-your-own-physics-engine hard."
A genuinely reflective Year-1 retrospective written for mentors wrestling with the same problems.
The 'Let's Talk About…' essays read like a column you'd subscribe to.

engineering-deep-dive.winner_citation: Turned a brownout post-mortem into a free engineering lecture, complete with the math to back it up.
engineering-deep-dive.quote: Browning out is when a robot's system voltage drops to a level where the Rio cuts off all outputs. The level is adjustable, I will use 7V for this discussion.
engineering-deep-dive.presenter: For the team that didn't just build it, but showed their work…

nominees.citation: Derived shooting-on-the-move from first principles instead of hand-waving it.
A turret breakdown deep enough that the student handed the calculus off to a mentor.
The CANJunction write-up doubles as a CAN-debugging masterclass.

best-cinematography.winner_citation: Thirty-eight videos in a single build thread — this wasn't a blog, it was a season-long documentary.
best-cinematography.quote: We've made massive progress on our design, and have even started some construction! Here's our completed full-robot CAD, up to this point.
best-cinematography.presenter: Roll the tape — and they had a lot of tape to roll…

nominees.citation: Over 1,400 images across the thread — a frame for every moment of the build.
Let the footage tell the story when the Geneva mechanism finally came alive.
Paired clean renders with video to make every update easy to follow.

best-comedy.winner_citation: A perfectly timed mid-post hand-off from student to mentor that earned its laugh honestly.
best-comedy.quote: It involved a lot of complex math and derivatives and stuff (I haven't done calculus yet :3), so I'll let the mentor explain later on this thread. Hi! It's me, "other mentor."
best-comedy.presenter: And the award for making the whole thread laugh without ever punching down…

nominees.citation: "Sometimes you just have to steal from the best" — a cheerfully honest homage to 254's Misfire.
Ran a 'Robots Done Quick' bit and fully committed to the speedrun energy.
"I promise I won't make any more puns" (they made more puns).

best-comeback.winner_citation: Went from 'cons: just about everything else' at Week 0 to the best event of their lives by DCMP — that's the arc.
best-comeback.quote: DCMP was the most fun and exhilarating event we've competed at ever - and probably will remain that for a long time to come.
best-comeback.presenter: For the team that took the rough first act and rewrote the ending…

nominees.citation: "We did what we could with what we had" — and came back with a plan.
Fought the triple-coax turret for weeks until the night it finally spun up.
The YAMS-ening: dragged a stubborn system from sim to a working bot.

most-helpful.winner_citation: Didn't just solve their own CAN headaches — battle-tested the fix across connector types so other teams wouldn't have to.
most-helpful.quote: We currently use the Molex SL compatible version, but have spent some time battle-testing both the WAGO Lever-Lock & Direct Solder versions of this board as well to ensure proper function for teams!
most-helpful.presenter: For the team whose best work this season was making everyone else's robot better…

nominees.citation: Published a full sponsorship guide so other teams could fund themselves.
Shared hopper simulations openly and credited the teams they learned from.
Posted their full kickoff game analysis for anyone trying to understand Rebuilt.
